things.name: Branicki Palace
things.description: Magnificent baroque palace from the 18th century known as the Versailles of Poland, with beautifully restored formal French gardens and state rooms

things.name: Bialystok Cathedral (St. Mary's Basilica)
things.description: Baroque basilica from the early 18th century with a magnificent interior, twin towers and status as the spiritual centre of the Bialystok archdiocese

things.name: Bialystok Market Square (Rynek Kosciuszki)
things.description: Historic main square with the 19th-century Town Hall and surrounding period buildings; the social heart of the city with cafes and seasonal events

things.name: Bialystok Opera and Philharmonic
things.description: Modern opera house and concert hall designed by Stefan Muller, hosting first-class opera, ballet and symphony performances throughout the year

things.name: Podlaskie Museum
things.description: Regional museum housed in the Branicki Palace complex with collections of fine art, archaeology and the cultural heritage of the Podlaskie region

things.name: Planty Park
things.description: Linear parkway encircling the old town with mature trees, fountains and promenading locals; a green buffer between the historic centre and modern city

things.name: Museum of the Bialystok Medical Academy
things.description: Unique museum telling the story of Ludwik Zamenhof, inventor of Esperanto, who was born in Bialystok in 1859; a fascinating linguistic pilgrimage

things.name: Suprasl Village
things.description: Charming village 15km east of Bialystok with an Orthodox monastery, wooden architecture and the Knyszynska Forest, ideal for a day trip

things.name: Gallery Arsenal
things.description: Contemporary art gallery in a renovated historic arsenal building presenting rotating exhibitions of Polish and international modern art

things.name: Miejski Stadion (Bialystok Stadium)
things.description: Home of Jagiellonia Bialystok, 2023-24 Polish Ekstraklasa champions and UEFA Conference League group stage participants
